title: Cerinthodes stylosum
taxonomy: Cerinthodes stylosum is a genus of flowering plants in the family Campanulaceae. The Campanulaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as bellflowers or bluebells. This family consists of around 89 genera and over 2,300 species. Cerinthodes stylosum specifically belongs to the tribe Lobelieae within the Campanulaceae family. The classification of Cerinthodes stylosum as a genus is relatively rare and may be subject to further research and classification updates.

intro: Cerinthodes stylosum, also known as the Blue Lobelia, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Campanulaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress, improve sleep, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. The bioactive constituents of Cerinthodes stylosum include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, including lobeline, which is a well-studied alkaloid with potential therapeutic applications. The most common herbal preparations made with Cerinthodes stylosum are teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support mental well-being.
